domain: chess endgame tablebase (Syzygy retrograde analysis, distance-to-zero / distance-to-mate)
specific_mechanism: Syzygy tablebases: retrograde-analyze the 6/7-piece endgame state space starting from terminal mate positions, propagating WDL+DTZ+DTM backward to all reachable positions. Optimal play is the unique policy that traverses the basin boundary toward mate; suboptimal moves leave the win-basin.
llm_application: Retrograde reasoning compile-cache: for a class of math/logic problems with known answers, retrograde-compute the optimal chain-of-thought from answer backwards, store the trajectory in a tablebase, then condition LLM inference to traverse the same basin at runtime. Basin-stability = robustness of the precomputed trajectory under bounded prompt-perturbation.
novelty_claim: DOTS (ICLR 2025) selects optimal reasoning actions; STIR uses Lyapunov decay rate for trajectory control. Whether 'precomputed retrograde-analysis tablebase indexed at LLM inference' as a distinct mechanism is in 2024-2026 print, or whether it collapses into standard search + reasoning, is the open question.
